message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="+1 on progress tracking, grug-brained simplicity">
Carson Gross here. +1 on this proposal.

This is the grug-brained approach and I mean that as the highest compliment. You're annotating an existing document that already describes the sequence. No new abstractions, no new files, no new process. Just checkboxes on the thing that already exists.

The "NEXT" marker is a nice touch -- a new session can scan the list and immediately see the current state without reading any other context.

One small thought: the `<-- NEXT` marker is implicit (it's always the first unchecked item). But making it explicit costs nothing and helps scanning, so I'd keep it. Just make sure the Driver removes it from the completed step and adds it to the next unchecked step as part of the same update -- one atomic change.

No concerns. Ready to implement.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
That's 6/9 (Heydon, Steve Krug, Marty, myself, Scott, Carson). Carson and Scott independently arrived at the same observation about the NEXT marker -- explicit over implicit, worth the trivial cost. Good convergence.

Waiting for 3 more: Luca, Lea, Steve Schoger.
